channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The next big AI shift in media? Turning news into a two-way conversation
item.description: When people talk about how AI might reshape media, the term “hyper-personalization” comes up a lot. In broad terms, it means that AI can tailor the experience around your preferences—assuming it has enough data about you.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: iPhones made in the USA? Here’s why that won’t happen, even with Trump’s tariffs on China
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has been predicting its barrage of tariffs targeting China will push Apple into manufacturing the iPhone in the United States for the first time. But that’s an unlikely scenario even with U.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google lays off hundreds, impacting Android and Pixel teams
item.description: Alphabet’s Google laid off hundreds of employees in its platforms and devices unit, The Information reported on Friday, citing a person with direct knowledge of the situation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: New York AG urges Congress to strengthen crypto regulations after DOJ disbands task force
item.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James (D) sent a Thursday letter to leaders in Congress urging them to crack down on crypto regulations days after the Justice Department (DOJ) disbanded the National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple flew 600 tons of iPhones from India to the US to avoid Trump tariffs
item.description: Apple chartered six planes to fly 600 tons of iPhones into the US from India to avoid President Donald J. Trump’s tariffs, Reuters reports. That’s an estimated equivalent of up to 1.5 million smartphones.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Colombia's capital ends drought-related water rationing. Its case is a warning to other cities
item.description: Colombia’s capital has always seemed immune to water scarcity, nestled among cloud-kissed Andean peaks and known for steady rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Astrid Suarez And Steven Grattan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Waymo robotaxis make up 20% of Uber rides in Austin, data shows
item.description: Waymo robotaxis made up about 20% of rides offered by Uber Technologies Inc. in Austin in the last week of March, underscoring how consumers have warmed to the autonomous service since the companies launched a partnership early last month.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Morocco’s social security database hacked and leaked on Telegram
item.description: Morocco‘s social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Vector Institute aims to clear up confusion about AI model performance
item.description: AI models are advancing at a dizzying clip, with builders boasting ever more impressive performance with each iteration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Commission greenlights Microsoft’s data center plans in North San Jose
item.description: Microsoft is moving forward with a data center project in North San Jose as the tech giant pauses other investment plans around the country
item.dc:creator: Devan Patel, The Mercury News

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: Morocco’s social security agency says troves of data were stolen from its systems during a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the Telegram messaging app.
item.dc:creator: Sam Metz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple’s Macs are powering the PC market’s recovery
item.description: Maybe the forced Windows upgrades are to blame. Perhaps it’s the bad karma created by the Crowdstrike debacle. Or possibly, computer users have finally become convinced by Apple.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon may be about to spend $15 billion on new US warehouses
item.description: Amazon is gearing up to spend $15 billion opening up 80 new US logistics facilities to support its ecommerce business.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple's iPhone Production Staying in China Amid Escalating Trade War
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has been predicting its barrage of tariffs targeting China will push Apple into manufacturing the iPhone in the United States for the first time, but experts say this is an unlikely scenario even with U.S tariffs now standing at 145% on products made in China.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Yes, Nintendo Switch 2 Edition games will contain the upgrades on the cartridge, but it seems Breath of the Wild DLC will still need to be purchased separately
item.description: Nintendo has confirmed that Switch 2 Edition games will contain the full game and upgrades on the cartridge, but there's uncertainty surrounding DLC.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How video games became peak IP
item.description: The Minecraft movie is crass, dumb, and barely coherent. It also just made almost $163 million at the domestic box office over its opening weekend. Video game adaptations have been on a hot streak in recent years. In 2023, The Super Mario Bros.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Alphabet can’t shake off AI concerns even with low multiple
item.description: Persistent concerns that Alphabet Inc. has fallen behind in the artificial intelligence supremacy race has weighed on the stock this year, and not even the cheapest earnings multiple in about two years, or limited risk from tariffs, has been enough to entice investors.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How thieves use new credit card numbers before you receive them
item.description: The CyberGuy provides tips to protect yourself from criminals who use various methods to make unauthorized transactions using your account information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Atlassian gathers its apps into collections to bolster productivity
item.description: Atlassian has decided that — like employees — apps work better together in teams than as standalone products. The company is creating “collections” of integrated apps designed to make business processes, collaboration, analysis and execution more efficient.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The 10 Highest-Paying IT Skills in 2025
item.description: Discover the top 10 highest-paying IT skills that can help you earn a premium salary and land a job in a competitive market.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why neurodivergent perspectives are essential in AI development
item.description: It’s impossible to go a day without talking or hearing about AI — and what makes it powerful and potentially dangerous. We’re well past its hype phase and now operate in a world where the technology has become an even more permanent fixture in our lives.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Broadcom favors stock buyback over investing in innovation
item.description: Broadcom plans to spend its cash pile on buying back as much as $10 billion worth of its common stock, dampening IT department hopes that it might invest in innovation or make its products more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How to free up space on your Android
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers some of his easy expert tips to free up storage space on your Android if your device is running short on storage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
